text: The only real stumbling block is fear of failure. In cooking you've got to have a what-the-hell attitude.
author: Julia Child

text: A recipe has no soul. You, as the cook, must bring soul to the recipe.
author: Thomas Keller

text: Good food is the foundation of genuine happiness.
author: Auguste Escoffier

text: Cooking is like painting or writing a song. Just as there are only so many notes or colors, there are only so many flavors.
author: Gordon Ramsay

text: Your body is not a temple, it's an amusement park. Enjoy the ride.
author: Anthony Bourdain

text: To me, there's no great chef without a great team.
author: Daniel Boulud

text: The most important class you can take is technique. A great chef is first a great technician.
author: Jacques Pépin

text: When you run a kitchen, no matter how crazy and chaotic it gets, you have to be the calm one.
author: Nina Compton

text: Cooking will never be good if it isn't made with love for the person it's being made for.
author: Paul Bocuse

text: When you acknowledge there is no such thing as perfect food, only the idea of it, then the real purpose of striving toward perfection becomes clear: to make people happy.
author: Thomas Keller

text: Chefs don't make mistakes; they make new dishes.
author: Elizabeth Brigg

text: Good food is a right, not a privilege.
author: Alice Waters
